name: Marjorie Taylor Greene
summary: Marjorie Taylor Greene's character and pattern of behavior are marked by a consistent promotion of conspiracy theories, Islamophobia, antisemitism, and white supremacy. She has supported calls to execute prominent Democratic politicians, compared COVID-19 safety measures to the Holocaust, and equated the Democratic Party with Nazis. Greene has also engaged in aggressive behavior, including confronting Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg and Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, and has been fined multiple times for refusing to wear a mask on the House floor. Her actions and statements demonstrate a disregard for facts, a willingness to spread misinformation, and a tendency to use violent and divisive rhetoric.

entries.fact: Marjorie Taylor Greene was a moderator of the Family America Project's Facebook group, where members posted death threats against Democrats and supported conspiracy theories.

Marjorie Taylor Greene visited the U.S. Capitol and congressional offices, where she posted livestream videos outside the offices of representatives, including Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Ilhan Omar, and Rashida Tlaib, making false claims and derogatory comments.
Marjorie Taylor Greene supported calls to execute prominent Democratic Party politicians, including Hillary Clinton and Barack Obama.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Islamophobic, antisemitic, and white supremacist views, including the white genocide conspiracy theory.

Marjorie Taylor Greene amplified conspiracy theories that allege government involvement in mass shootings in the United States, implicate the Clinton family in murder, and suggest the attacks of 9/11 were a hoax.

Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a gun rights rally in Villanow hosted by Silent No Longer, a group campaigning to make Walker County a Second Amendment sanctuary, and held up a banner for American Patriots USA, a far-right group.
Marjorie Taylor Greene posed for photos with Chester Doles, a 5th-generation Ku Klux Klansman and Grand Klaliff leader, at a gun rights rally in Villanow.

Marjorie Taylor Greene finished first in the June 9 primary, and a Facebook video of hers was taken down for violating its terms of service, in which she held an AR-15 style rifle and warned antifa groups to 'stay the hell out of Northwest Georgia'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's company, Taylor Commercial, received $182,300 in PPP funding from the Small Business Administration during the COVID-19 pandemic, and around two months later, Greene donated $450,000 to her campaign.

Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote on Twitter that children should not wear masks, calling recommendations by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and other public health officials 'unhealthy for their psychological, emotional, and educational growth' and 'emasculating' for boys.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called restrictions imposed in the U.S. Capitol in response to the COVID-19 pandemic, including face mask requirements, 'tyrannical control' by Democrats.

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a meme to her Facebook page depicting herself holding an AR-15 style rifle next to a collage of pictures of Ocasio-Cortez, Omar, and Tlaib, which was later deleted by Facebook for violating its policies on inciting violence.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi 'anti-American', a 'hypocrite', and a 'bitch' during her victory speech
Marjorie Taylor Greene had a reporter from The Atlanta Journal-Constitution ousted from the venue after he tweeted quotes from her speech
Marjorie Taylor Greene made deceptive claims about her husband's voting record, suggesting voter fraud
Marjorie Taylor Greene equated the Democratic Party with Nazis and compared COVID-19 safety measures to the persecution of Jews during the Holocaust.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was part of a group of Republican legislators who unsuccessfully challenged votes for Joe Biden during the 2021 United States Electoral College vote count.
Marjorie Taylor Greene raised an objection to counting Michigan's electoral votes during the counting of electoral votes
Marjorie Taylor Greene wore a face mask onto the House floor that read 'Trump Won', despite Trump having lost the 2020 U.S. presidential election
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a face mask while sheltering in place during the riot at the US Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that Democrats must be held accountable for the political violence inspired by their rhetoric during the debate to impeach Trump.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely suggested that the rioters at the US Capitol were members of antifa dressed as Trump supporters.
The U.S. House of Representatives voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from all committee roles in response to her endorsements of political violence.
Marjorie Taylor Greene faced a legal challenge to her eligibility based on her alleged involvement in organizing and promoting the 2021 United States Capitol attack

The Federal Election Commission asked Marjorie Taylor Greene's 2022 re-election campaign treasurer to provide information about $3.5 million of unitemized campaign contributions from undisclosed donors
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted Russian propaganda and praised its president Vladimir Putin during the Russian invasion of Ukraine.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was appointed to new committee roles.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the conservative House Freedom Caucus after insulting fellow caucus member Congresswoman Lauren Boebert.
Marjorie Taylor Greene unsuccessfully attempted to oust Mike Johnson from his role as Speaker of the House of Representatives.
Marjorie Taylor Greene announced her resignation from Congress effective January 5, 2026, stating that she did not want her district to endure a 'hurtful and hateful primary'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene confronted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg near the U.S. Capitol, questioning his gun control activism and calling him a coward for ignoring her.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that David Hogg's gun control activism was funded by billionaire George Soros.
Marjorie Taylor Greene opposed any form of mandatory mask-wearing, compulsory vaccination, or lockdowns in response to the pandemic.
Marjorie Taylor Greene refused to wear a mask in a secured room with other members of Congress during the storming of the Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene filed articles of impeachment against President Joe Biden, citing alleged abuse of power as the reason for impeachment.
Representative Jimmy Gomez announced he had drafted a resolution to expel Marjorie Taylor Greene from the House after reports that she had previously called for violence against Democrats.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was criticized by Pelosi for her threats of violence against fellow lawmakers and for wanting to bring guns onto the House floor.
Representatives Nikema Williams and Sara Jacobs introduced a resolution to censure Marjorie Taylor Greene for making threatening comments to her colleagues and to call for her resignation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was removed from all committee assignments due to incendiary remarks she had made before her election.
Representative Jimmy Gomez formally introduced his resolution to expel Marjorie Taylor Greene, with the support of 72 Democrats and no Republicans.
Marjorie Taylor Greene cast a lone vote against a bill to reauthorize the National Marrow Donor Program, falsely claiming that the bill would fund aborted fetal tissue.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the January 6 commission, a proposed commission which would have investigated the attack on the Capitol.

Marjorie Taylor Greene called for justice for Ashli Babbitt, a Trump supporter who was fatally shot by a Capitol police officer when she stormed the Capitol and attempted to break into the House chamber.

Marjorie Taylor Greene and Gaetz initiated a nationwide 'America First Tour', beginning in The Villages, Florida, where they repeated unsubstantiated claims of fraud purported to have occurred during the 2020 election.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the Plan B contraceptive 'kills a baby in the womb', when in fact it prevents ovulation and thus prevents pregnancy.

Marjorie Taylor Greene aggressively confronted Ocasio-Cortez outside the House chamber, loudly asking why she supported antifa and Black Lives Matter, which Greene falsely characterized as terrorists.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was fined $500 for not wearing a mask on the House floor.

Marjorie Taylor Greene called Nancy Pelosi 'mentally ill' and compared her requirement that House members continue wearing masks until they all prove they have been vaccinated to the Holocaust, saying it was 'exactly the [same] type of abuse' as Jews being 'put in trains and taken to gas chambers in Nazi Germany'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene incurred a second fine of $2,500 after she again entered the House floor without a mask.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was one of 21 House Republicans to vote against a resolution to give the Congressional Gold Medal to police officers who defended the U.S. Capitol on January 6.
Marjorie Taylor Greene sent Biden a letter calling for an investigation into Fauci over his statements on the origin of SARS-CoV-2
Marjorie Taylor Greene publicly apologized for her comments comparing COVID-19 vaccine policies to the Holocaust after a private visit to the Holocaust Museum in Washington, D.C.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Biden's suggestion to provide door-to-door vaccinations to 'medical brownshirts'
Marjorie Taylor Greene promised that telecommunications companies that complied with the House Select Committee on the January 6 Attack's request to retain phone records would be 'shut down'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene owned stocks in three COVID-19 vaccine manufacturers, including Pfizer, Johnson & Johnson, and AstraZeneca, despite previously expressing opposition to COVID-19 vaccines.
Marjorie Taylor Greene had been fined 20 times for not wearing a mask on the House floor, totaling $48,000 in fines
Marjorie Taylor Greene spoke at a conference hosted by white supremacist Nick Fuentes, where Fuentes called the January 6 attack 'awesome' and praised Putin and Hitler.
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced criticisms of her attendance at a white supremacist conference as 'identity politics' and an attempt to 'cancel' her, and labelled critics 'Pharisees'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene was expelled from the Freedom Caucus by a vote of its members after calling fellow caucus member Lauren Boebert a 'little bitch' during an argument on the House floor.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a pair of resolutions to expunge both of Trump's impeachments.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said Nancy Pelosi is guilty of treason and that treason is a crime punishable by death in a Facebook video.
Marjorie Taylor Greene visited Nancy Pelosi's office and suggested that Pelosi would either be killed or imprisoned for treason in Facebook live-streamed videos.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said she would always protect gun owners' rights and would not vote for any laws making it harder for people to possess guns
Marjorie Taylor Greene defended her actions in a 2019 video where she confronted David Hogg, despite backlash from Parkland activists.
Marjorie Taylor Greene opposed foreign aid, saying that American tax dollars should only go to America, not to other countries
Marjorie Taylor Greene called for the results of the 2020 U.S. presidential election in Georgia to be decertified
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced an article of impeachment against Biden, with no co-sponsors
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against a measure condemning the Myanmar coup d'état
Marjorie Taylor Greene argued that Canada's proposed gun control laws would leave Canada weak and vulnerable to being invaded and easily taken over by another stronger country.
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced a bill to abolish the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives.
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted to repeal the Authorization for Use of Military Force Against Iraq Resolution of 2002
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to COVID-19 as a 'manufactured plague' caused by a 'Chinese-made virus'
Marjorie Taylor Greene dismissed the COVID-19 variants, including the Delta variant, saying 'no one cares'
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted misinformation about the virus, saying it 'is not dangerous for non-obese people and those under 65'
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated she would kick out every Chinese person in the United States who is loyal to the Chinese Communist Party and impose strict tariffs on China if she had the authority
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted against the bipartisan ALLIES Act, which would increase the number of special immigrant visas for Afghan allies of the U.S. military
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Biden would be 'sending one of his police state friends to your front door' to inquire whether they had received a vaccine
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely said that hospitals were not overwhelmed with afflicted patients
Marjorie Taylor Greene purchased shares of Digital World Acquisition Corp., a special purpose acquisition company that had just merged with Trump Media & Technology Group
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that any Republican who supported the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act was a traitor to their party, voters, and donors.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the July 4 Highland Park parade shooting was designed to make Republicans go along with more gun control.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the July 4 Highland Park parade shooting occurred in a rich, white neighborhood and implied that it was a conspiracy to promote gun control.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the anti-vaccine film Died Suddenly, which promotes misinformation about COVID-19 vaccines and Great Reset conspiracy theories
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted in favor of H.Con.Res. 21, which directed President Biden to remove U.S. troops from Syria within 180 days
Marjorie Taylor Greene stated that lax security at the Mexico–United States border contributed to the opioid epidemic in the United States
Marjorie Taylor Greene sponsored a resolution to censure Democratic U.S. representative Rashida Tlaib for alleged 'antisemitic activity' and 'sympathizing' with Hamas
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed an amendment to a House foreign aid bill on Ukraine that would require all members of the House voting in favor of the bill to enlist in the Ukrainian military
Marjorie Taylor Greene repeatedly refused to address Fauci as Doctor and questioned his medical licence during a congressional hearing
Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed a defense appropriations amendment that would end U.S. funding of Israel's Iron Dome
Marjorie Taylor Greene voted in favor of the amendment to end U.S. funding of Israel's Iron Dome
Marjorie Taylor Greene speculated that the Pizzagate conspiracy theory is real and suggested John F. Kennedy Jr.'s death in a plane crash in 1999 was a 'Clinton murder'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed doubt that the perpetrator of the 2017 Las Vegas shooting acted alone, suggesting it was an attack on the right to bear arms.
Marjorie Taylor Greene compared Black Lives Matter activists to white nationalist participants at the August 2017 Unite the Right rally in Charlottesville, Virginia
Marjorie Taylor Greene said the most mistreated group of people in the United States today are white males
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the 2017 Unite the Right rally an inside job
Marjorie Taylor Greene told viewers that 'the only way you get your freedoms back is it's earned with the price of blood' in an interview with gun activist Chris Dorr.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the Squad supporters of terrorism and Hamas who don't belong in Congress and referred to them as the Jihad Squad
Marjorie Taylor Greene said she would love to trade Katie Hopkins for some of our white people here that have no appreciation for our country
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the verdict in the Derek Chauvin trial was a result of jury intimidation by Black Lives Matter
Marjorie Taylor Greene denounced the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act as an Anti-Police bill and erroneously claimed it would bar law enforcement from using facial recognition

Marjorie Taylor Greene rejected the scientific consensus that climate change is caused primarily by human activity, saying 'maybe perhaps we live on a ball that rotates around the sun, that flies through the universe, and maybe our climate just changes'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene was found to have past ties with extremist militia groups, including the Three Percenters and the Oath Keepers, which had members participate in the 2021 attack on the U.S. Capitol.
Marjorie Taylor Greene co-sponsored the Old Glory Only Bill, a bill to ban U.S. embassies from flying pride flags.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said she was censored by Twitter for believing that God created people male and female.
Marjorie Taylor Greene tried to block the Equality Act, saying it would destroy God's creation and violate everything held dear in God's creation.

Marjorie Taylor Greene proposed replacing the Equality Act with a bill that would exempt nonprofit organizations, allow people to sue the federal government for violating their religious rights, and prevent trans women and girls from participating in women's sports.
Marjorie Taylor Greene displayed a transphobic poster outside her office, reading: There are TWO genders: Male & Female. Trust The Science!
Marjorie Taylor Greene supported a bill that would bar schools from allowing transgender students to use facilities such as bathrooms and locker rooms that correspond to their gender identity.

Marjorie Taylor Greene was involved in launching an America First Caucus, which believes that America is a nation with a border, and a culture, strengthened by a common respect for uniquely Anglo-Saxon political traditions
Marjorie Taylor Greene called transgender women men playing dress up and having an identity crisis, saying they will never be women.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that schools teaching about LGBT people is mental/emotional child abuse.
Marjorie Taylor Greene called the $1.2 trillion Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act Biden's communist takeover of America.

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the 13 House Republicans who voted for the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, calling them American job and energy killers and deriding them as China-First and America-Last.
Marjorie Taylor Greene falsely claimed that the perpetrator of the 2022 Robb Elementary School shooting in Texas was transgender
Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized Ketanji Brown Jackson for not answering Senator Marsha Blackburn's question what is a woman?, and said that women came from Adam's rib and were created by God.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that straight people would go extinct within less than two centuries due to LGBT-inclusive educators, calling them trans terrorists.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that an entire pride month celebrating LGBT people needs to end
Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced the Protect Children's Innocence Act, which would make giving gender-affirming care to transgender youth a felony punishable by up to 25 years in prison
Marjorie Taylor Greene responded to California state senator Scott Wiener by saying the Protect Children's Innocence Act would stop communist groomers such as Wiener
An updated version of Marjorie Taylor Greene's Protect Children's Innocence Act passed the House by a vote of 216-211
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the August 2017 Charlottesville white nationalist rally was an 'inside job'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for the execution of leading Democrats, including Pelosi, Barack Obama, and Hillary Clinton, as well as support for the execution of FBI agents.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's Facebook account responded to a post asking about hanging Democrats, saying 'Stage is being set. Players are being put in place. We must be patient. This must be done perfectly or liberal judges would let them off.'
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed support for a conspiracy theory that a plane did not hit the Pentagon during the September 11 attacks.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said 9/11 was part of a U.S. government plot.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Democratic National Committee staff member Seth Rich was murdered by the MS-13 gang on Obama's behalf.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that Obama is secretly a Muslim.

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a video promoting the white genocide conspiracy theory, which has been associated with white supremacy and espouses the unsubstantiated belief that white people will eventually become a minority in Europe and North America due to declining white birth rates and high rates of immigration.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the conspiracy theory that George Soros' family collaborated with the Nazis in Hungary and is 'trying to continue what was not finished'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene shared a conspiracy theory about the Camp Fire, a deadly Californian wildfire, suggesting that it could have been caused by 'what looked like lasers or beams of blue light' from 'space solar generators' in a scheme involving California governor Jerry Brown, companies PG&E, Rothschild & Co, and Solaren.
Marjorie Taylor Greene suggested that Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg had been replaced in public appearances by a body double.
Marjorie Taylor Greene followed and taunted Parkland shooting survivor David Hogg, accusing him of using children for his cause.

Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted multiple conspiracy theories, including the claim that Hillary Clinton is responsible for a series of murders, that Democratic Party elites are responsible for a satanic child sex trafficking ring, and that the government orchestrated the 2017 Las Vegas shooting.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed racist, antisemitic, and Islamophobic views in videos published before the Republican primary runoff election.
Marjorie Taylor Greene expressed racist, antisemitic, and Islamophobic views in videos published before the Republican primary runoff election.
Marjorie Taylor Greene wrote on Twitter that she now knows it's not correct that a missile hit the Pentagon, after previously expressing doubts about the 9/11 attacks.

Marjorie Taylor Greene advocated a 'national divorce' between red states and blue states and suggested that red states disenfranchise people who move there from blue states for a period of five years.

Marjorie Taylor Greene agreed with a conspiracy theory known as Frazzledrip, which asserts that there is a video of Hillary Clinton and her assistant Huma Abedin murdering a child in a satanic ritual.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2018 Stoneman Douglas High School shooting in Parkland, Florida, was an organized false flag operation.
Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed that the 2012 Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a false flag operation.
A staffer for Marjorie Taylor Greene threatened to have a reporter arrested after the reporter tried to ask Greene a question about her harassment of David Hogg.
Marjorie Taylor Greene defended Christian nationalism and described herself as a 'proud Christian nationalist'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene promoted the conspiracy theory that the U.S. government is planning to force Americans to eat fake meat grown in a 'peach tree dish' by Bill Gates.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that the Robb Elementary School shooter had engaged in cross-dressing, echoing similar claims by right-wing politicians and media figures that the shooter was transgender.

Marjorie Taylor Greene said 'People die in the cold. This Earth warming, and carbon, is actually healthy for us. It helps us to feed people, it keeps people alive. ... We need to hold Democrats accountable and defund all of their climate garbage' in an interview.
Marjorie Taylor Greene said that Democrats had designed the killing of Ayman al-Zawahiri to boost Biden in the midterms because the Russian invasion of Ukraine had not achieved this.

Marjorie Taylor Greene tweeted that climate change was a 'scam' and that 'fossil fuels are natural and amazing', saying that 'there are some very powerful people that are getting rich beyond their wildest dreams convincing many that carbon is the enemy'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene's tweet included a chart that omitted carbon dioxide and methane, the two most dominant greenhouse gas emissions.

Marjorie Taylor Greene posted online a map of hurricane-affected areas with an overlay of electoral map by political party, showing many Republican-leaning areas affected, and wrote 'Yes they can control the weather … It's ridiculous for anyone to lie and say it can't be done'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene's Twitter account was temporarily suspended five times for violating Twitter's policies, including spreading COVID-19 vaccine misinformation
Marjorie Taylor Greene posted false claims about voting fraud and statements blaming electoral officials in Georgia on Twitter before her account was locked.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's personal Twitter account was locked for 12 hours for multiple violations of Twitter's civic integrity policy, including false claims about voting fraud and statements blaming electoral officials in Georgia.
House majority leader Steny Hoyer gave Republican House leaders an ultimatum to strip Greene of her committee seats within 72 hours, or the Democrats would bring the motion before the full House.

Marjorie Taylor Greene's behavior was described as 'appalling' by Representative Debbie Wasserman Schultz, who introduced a motion to remove Greene from her committee assignments due to her promotion of conspiracy theories and incendiary statements.
McCarthy called some of Greene's comments 'deeply disturbing'.
Senate Minority Leader Mitch McConnell issued a statement attacking 'loony lies and conspiracy theories' as a 'cancer for the Republican Party', which was widely seen as targeted at Greene.
Greene tweeted that the Republican Party's only problem are 'weak Republicans who only know how to lose gracefully' in response to McConnell's statement.
Kevin Cramer said that he would have 'a hard time supporting ... [Greene] being on the Education Committee' due to her positions on school shootings being staged.
Mitt Romney said that the Republican Party's 'big tent is not large enough to both accommodate conservatives and kooks', in reference to Greene.
Greene used the criticism for a fundraising push, saying 'Democrats are trying to expel me from Congress' and telling her supporters to 'StandWithMTG'.
Marco Rubio said that anyone arguing that the Stoneman Douglas High School shooting was a false flag is 'either deranged or a sadist', in reference to Greene's comments.
The full House voted to remove Marjorie Taylor Greene from her committee assignments with a vote of 230 to 199
Marjorie Taylor Greene used the word 'retard' to refer to individuals with Down syndrome and later apologized for being offensive
Marjorie Taylor Greene settled out of court with MeidasTouch, a political action committee she had blocked from posting on her Twitter page, and was required to pay $10,000 in legal fees.
Common Cause filed an FEC complaint against Marjorie Taylor Greene for an alleged violation of McCain–Feingold campaign finance provisions

The White House criticized Marjorie Taylor Greene for attacking its student loan forgiveness program, calling her hypocritical since she accepted forgiveness of a $183,504 loan from the Paycheck Protection Program
Marjorie Taylor Greene opposed the Equality Act.
Marjorie Taylor Greene referred to the police guarding the Capitol building as 'gazpacho police', confusing the cold Spanish soup with the Gestapo.
Marjorie Taylor Greene accused Republicans who supported then-Supreme Court nominee Ketanji Brown Jackson of being 'pro-pedophile'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene denied allegations that she had been involved in assisting the 2021 United States Capitol attack.

Marjorie Taylor Greene filed a federal lawsuit to block a state law that allowed a candidate to be removed from a ballot after review by an administrative law judge and the Georgia Secretary of State.

A federal judge denied Marjorie Taylor Greene's challenge to a state law that allowed a candidate to be removed from a ballot after review by an administrative law judge and the Georgia Secretary of State.

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the United States Conference of Catholic Bishops, accusing them of 'destroying' America with their work regarding immigrants and refugees and claiming that Satan was 'controlling the church'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed without evidence that the Robb Elementary School shooter was in the same Discord server as the teen accused of carrying out the 2022 Buffalo shooting and that there was a third party who 'groomed' both young men.
A judge ruled that Marjorie Taylor Greene was eligible for reelection, but the final decision belonged to Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger.
Twitter labeled two tweets by Marjorie Taylor Greene as violating their rules on hateful conduct for misgendering and deadnaming Assistant Secretary for Health Rachel Levine.

Marjorie Taylor Greene criticized the presence of the Georgia Guidestones, a 19-foot high megalithic granite monument, and said that the ecumenical texts inscribed on it represented a nefarious future of 'population control' as envisioned by the 'hard left'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene targeted and misgendered congresswoman Sarah McBride in a series of tweets, labeling her a 'groomer'.
Marjorie Taylor Greene indicated support for executing prominent Democrats
Marjorie Taylor Greene entered the 6th District GOP race with a pledge to cut spending, which may be considered a policy that could cause harm to vulnerable groups if implemented.
Marjorie Taylor Greene had a social media post removed for inciting violence.
Marjorie Taylor Greene spread false information about activists starting Oregon fires on social media.
Marjorie Taylor Greene spread conspiracies about Charlottesville and 'Pizzagate'

Marjorie Taylor Greene was described as a 'conspiracy theorist' by multiple sources, including The Guardian, Associated Press, and Axios, due to her promotion of QAnon conspiracy theories.

Marjorie Taylor Greene launched a new 'America First' caucus, pushing for 'Anglo-Saxon political tradition'.

Marjorie Taylor Greene compared vaccine rules to the Holocaust, prompting criticism from GOP lawmakers
